name: plan-how-to-build
title: Technical Design Doc
description: Use when turning approved requirements into a buildable technical design with explicit boundaries, data and control flow, decisions, trade-offs, and verification paths. Do not use for product briefs, IA maps, or pure feature requirements.
purpose: Translate approved product and feature intent into a buildable technical design that makes boundaries, flows, decisions, and verification explicit.
when_to_use: Need a technical design before implementation starts.
Need to make component boundaries, data flow, and trade-offs explicit.
Need one design document that engineers can implement against and review.
do_not_use: Need only high-level product framing or user problem definition.
Need only feature behavior, scope, and acceptance scenarios.
Need only information hierarchy or navigation structure.
Need only bounded dependency direction rules without a broader technical mechanism.

required_inputs.name: IMPLEMENTATION_GOAL
required_inputs.description: Core behavior or capability the design must enable.

required_inputs.name: REQUIREMENT_SOURCES
required_inputs.description: Briefs, specs, or contracts that the design must satisfy.

required_inputs.name: SYSTEM_BOUNDARIES
required_inputs.description: Known components or boundaries that must appear in the design.

required_inputs.name: CONSTRAINTS
required_inputs.description: Compatibility, performance, migration, policy, or operational constraints.

required_inputs.name: ROLLBACK_OR_MIGRATION
required_inputs.description: Rollback, migration, or rollout-sensitive steps that affect the design.

input_contract_notes: REQUIREMENT_SOURCES should point to explicit upstream artifacts instead of relying on memory or broad verbal summaries.
SYSTEM_BOUNDARIES may be partial, but the final design must still surface the real responsibilities it introduces or touches.
CONSTRAINTS should name non-negotiable realities such as latency budgets, compatibility rules, or migration limits.
Use ROLLBACK_OR_MIGRATION only when it materially shapes the design, not as a catch-all implementation checklist.

structured_outputs.name: DESIGN_SUMMARY
structured_outputs.description: One concise summary of the proposed design and its central trade-off.

structured_outputs.name: BOUNDARY_MAP
structured_outputs.description: Components or boundaries and the responsibility each one owns.

structured_outputs.name: DATA_AND_CONTROL_FLOW
structured_outputs.description: Important data or control flows that make the design understandable.

structured_outputs.name: DECISIONS_AND_TRADEOFFS
structured_outputs.description: Key design decisions and what each decision costs or protects.

structured_outputs.name: RISKS_AND_VERIFICATION
structured_outputs.description: Main implementation or rollout risks and the check that reduces each one.

structured_outputs.name: MIGRATION_AND_ROLLBACK
structured_outputs.description: Migration or rollback-sensitive steps that the design must respect.

output_contract_notes: BOUNDARY_MAP should identify real ownership boundaries rather than generic layer names with no responsibilities.
DATA_AND_CONTROL_FLOW should make the main mechanism visible enough that reviewers can reason about correctness and cost.
DECISIONS_AND_TRADEOFFS should describe what was chosen and what was given up, not just restate the happy path.
RISKS_AND_VERIFICATION should connect each risk to the cheapest meaningful check.
neutrality_rules: Separate upstream requirements from the chosen solution mechanism.
Do not invent architectural complexity when a simpler mechanism satisfies the stated constraints.
If a design decision cannot be justified from the sources and constraints, mark it as an open decision instead of pretending it is settled.
execution_constraints: Do not restate the entire product brief or feature spec; focus on how the system will satisfy the approved contract.
Do not reduce this skill to a dependency-rule checklist when the only missing artifact is target dependency direction.
Prefer the smallest design that satisfies the requirement sources and explicit constraints.
Keep boundaries, side effects, and trade-offs explicit instead of hiding them in polished prose.
default_program:
[stages: preflight>detect>analyze>plan>review>handoff>audit |
 scope: repo|paths(glob,...) |
 policy: evidence,quality-gates{docs,compat},deterministic-output |
 lens: hickey-carmack |
 output: md(contract=v1)]
example_invocation:
$plan-how-to-build
DESIGN_SCOPE: feature
IMPLEMENTATION_GOAL: 비밀번호 재설정 토큰 검증과 새 비밀번호 저장을 안전하게 처리한다
REQUIREMENT_SOURCES:
  - REF: docs/specs/password-reset.md
    WHY_RELEVANT: 기능 요구사항과 예외 조건의 기준
CONSTRAINTS:
  - CONSTRAINT: 기존 사용자 테이블 스키마는 유지한다
lens_rationale: Technical design docs should make boundaries, data flow, side effects, and trade-offs explicit enough that implementation can stay simple and reviewable.
